purpose: Maps substantive CCPCNC variables to 14 constitutional dimensions for typology construction. Structural, metadata, source, and symbolic variables are excluded.
how_to_combine: Concatenate the 'variables' array from each part file. All entries share the same schema.

entry_schema.variable_name: Canonical variable name (parent name for multi-response questions). Sub-variables follow the pattern PARENT_N where N is the answer code.
entry_schema.question_number: vN — the survey instrument question number from the CCP codebook
entry_schema.description: Human-readable explanation of what the variable measures
entry_schema.coding_scheme: Full set of values from the codebook. For multi-response questions, lists all sub-variable names and what each measures. Each sub-variable is binary: 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable.
entry_schema.dimensions: Array of one or more dimension keys the variable is relevant to
entry_schema.notes: Optional. Flags borderline assignments, inter-variable dependencies, or direction of coding relative to dimension

special_codes_universal.98: Not Specified (topic not mentioned in constitution)
special_codes_universal.99: Not Applicable (prior question makes this question irrelevant)

dimensions.civil_liberties: Constitutional protections for individual freedoms such as speech, assembly, religion, movement, privacy, and freedom from arbitrary arrest or detention.
dimensions.socioeconomic_rights: Provisions guaranteeing rights related to education, healthcare, housing, social security, labor (e.g., fair wages, unionization), and an adequate standard of living.
dimensions.political_competition: Rules governing multi-party systems, free and fair elections, suffrage, referendums, and the ability of opposition parties to form and compete for power.
dimensions.legislative_autonomy: The legislature's independence from executive control, including powers to initiate legislation, oversee the executive, control its own procedures, and resist dissolution or interference.
dimensions.executive_constraints: Constitutional limits on executive power, such as term limits, veto override procedures, legislative or judicial checks, and requirements for approval of appointments or treaties.
dimensions.judicial_independence: Provisions insulating judges from political pressure, including security of tenure, protected salaries, immunity from removal except for cause, and autonomous court administration.
dimensions.rule_of_law_due_process: Constitutional guarantees that laws are applied equally and consistently, including due process rights (notice, hearing, impartial tribunal), prohibition of retroactive laws, and habeas corpus.
dimensions.institutional_accountability: Mechanisms that hold state institutions accountable, such as ombudsmen, anti-corruption commissions, supreme audit institutions, and independent central banks.
dimensions.emergency_powers_constraints: Limits on the declaration and exercise of states of emergency, including parliamentary oversight, sunset clauses, non-derogable rights, and judicial review of emergency measures.
dimensions.civilian_control_of_security: Provisions ensuring the military, police, and intelligence services are subordinate to elected civilian authorities, such as civilian defense ministers, prohibitions on military roles in law enforcement, and legislative oversight.
dimensions.amendment_rigidity: The procedural difficulty of amending the constitution, measured by requirements such as supermajorities in the legislature, multiple sessions, referendums, or approval by subnational units.
dimensions.federalism_decentralization: Constitutional distribution of power between central and subnational governments, including enumerated state/provincial powers, fiscal autonomy, local self-government, and mechanisms for intergovernmental cooperation.
dimensions.transparency_information_access: Provisions guaranteeing public access to government documents and meetings, subject only to limited exceptions (national security, privacy), and establishing a right to information.
dimensions.equality_gender_minority_indigenous: Constitutional protections against discrimination based on gender, ethnicity, religion, disability, sexual orientation, or indigenous status, including affirmative action, group rights, or special representation.

parts.label: Judiciary — All Courts, Review, Removal, Independence
Rights — Citizenship, Rule of Law, Information & Religion
Rights — Economic, Property, Labor & Social Security
Special Domains — Military, Education, Ethnicity/Language, Environment

note_on_dataset_columns: Many entries correspond to multiple columns in the actual CCPCNC dataset because multi-response questions generate one binary column per answer choice. Total dataset columns covered is approximately 400+.
